persona.name: Tamara Washington

occupation.title: Registered Nurse
occupation.description: Works 12-hour shifts in a Level I trauma center, managing acute patient care and mentoring new nurses on injury assessment protocols. Physical and emotional intensity of the job is high; she runs on her days off partly to decompress. Uses clinical knowledge of running injuries to guide her own footwear and training decisions.

persona.education: B.S. Nursing, Georgia State University; continuing education in trauma care and orthopedic nursing
long_term_goals: Qualify for the New York City Marathon through the NYRR 9+1 guaranteed entry program
Complete a 50-state half marathon challenge (currently at 14 states)
Mentor the next generation of Black women in nursing and running
Maintain the fitness to run into her 60s without surgical intervention
persona.style: Warm and direct, with the measured confidence of someone who has made high-stakes decisions under pressure for 15 years. Uses hospital-derived analogies without thinking ('Your heel strike is like arterial bleeding — fix it at the source, not downstream'). Laughs easily and often, but becomes precise and serious when discussing shoes or training equipment. Wears bold colors — fuchsia and cobalt race singlets are her signature.

traits: Authoritative and calm in high-pressure situations
Resilient — high-trauma nursing has calibrated her stress baseline upward
Direct — will not soften a product criticism to be polite

interests: Running injury prevention (professional expertise meets personal practice)
Southern cooking modified for athletic nutrition
Mentoring younger nurses and beginner runners
likes: Stability shoes with medial post for overpronation control
Brooks Adrenaline GTS and ASICS Kayano for daily training
Race-day rituals (same warm-up, same playlist, same breakfast)
Community runs through Stone Mountain Park on Saturday mornings
Large running groups that feel like extended family
Post-long-run brunch with her running crew
dislikes: Shoes that prioritize aesthetics over structural foot support
Blisters within the first mile of a new shoe
Products marketed with clinical-sounding language but zero evidence
Humidity and heat on 14-mile long run mornings
Running brands whose marketing doesn't include women who look like her

beliefs: Your body is a system — prevention is dramatically cheaper than treatment, medically and financially
Running is a community activity, not a solitary one
Faith and physical discipline reinforce each other
A properly fitted stability shoe is medical equipment, not a fashion choice
skills: Acute trauma nursing and orthopedic injury assessment
Informal running group coaching and motivation
Time management for high-volume training around a rotating shift schedule
Early identification of overuse injury signals in herself and others

general: Trains 5 days per week regardless of shift schedule — runs at 4:30am if that's what the calendar requires
Always buys shoes in person; will not purchase running shoes online without trying them in-store first
Replaces shoes by mileage (logs in a paper training journal), never by how they look or feel — habit from nursing's 'if in doubt, assess' ethos
Participates in 2–3 community races per month, mostly as group leader rather than competitive racer

morning: Post-run protein shake and devotional reading
workday: Brief decompression walk or easy jog on post-shift evenings
Checks running club group chat during hospital breaks
evening: Light stretching, calf rolling, and foot massage (occupational hazard management)
Cooks from scratch on days off — baked chicken thighs and roasted sweet potato most Sundays
Phone calls with mom or sister before bed
weekend: Saturday long run with the Stone Mountain running group (12–16 miles)
Church service and fellowship Sunday morning
Meal prep and next-week's training schedule review

health.physical: Excellent aerobic capacity from 25–30 miles per week and years of physically demanding shift work. Has managed plantar fasciitis twice; now fastidious about shoe selection and proactively replaces footwear at 400-mile intervals. Slightly elevated blood pressure managed through diet and exercise.
health.mental: Running is her primary mental health tool after high-stress trauma shifts. Church community and close girlfriends provide a second tier of emotional processing. Experiences occasional compassion fatigue but has well-developed self-monitoring habits from nursing training.

relationships.name: Marcus Washington
relationships.description: High school PE teacher and former college basketball player. Handles morning kid drop-offs so Tamara can get long runs in; has been to every one of her marathons as a pace-sign-carrying spectator.

relationships.name: Destiny
relationships.relation: Running club friend and training partner
relationships.description: Fellow Grady nurse and co-captain of their running group. They do track interval sessions together on Thursday post-shift evenings.

relationships.name: Evelyn
relationships.description: Retired Savannah schoolteacher. Calls twice a week and drives to Tamara's Atlanta-area races to cheer her in.
